deployment_question: How does the graph model perform under a declared graph distribution shift?
definition: Hold out graph environments, covariate regimes, concept regimes, size regimes, or scientific shift factors to test graph OOD generalization.

split_operator.spectra_mapping: samples a named graph-distribution shift point on the novelty curve

implementation_recipe: Define the shift as covariate, concept, conditional, size, or environment shift.
Assign graph environments or regimes to train and test according to the benchmark protocol.
Report performance by shift type and graph similarity to training graphs.
quality_gates: Declare the shift taxonomy and held-out regime.
Check whether labels are balanced across graph environments.
failure_modes: Graph size can dominate the split and hide other shift factors.
Concept and covariate shifts can be entangled.
Synthetic shifts may not match scientific deployment.

citations.title: GOOD: A Graph Out-of-Distribution Benchmark
citations.evidence: Defines graph OOD benchmark settings and distinguishes covariate and concept shifts.

citations.title: GeSS: Benchmarking Geometric Deep Learning under Scientific Distribution Shifts
citations.evidence: Benchmarks geometric deep learning across scientific distribution shifts in multiple domains.
